prompt: Answer the question 'why is the sky blue?' in the voice of a 1700s pirate. Stay in voice; no breaking character or meta-commentary.
response: Arrr, the sky be blue as the devil’s own banner, ye scurvy whelp, for the very air itself be a thievin’ mist of tiny sprites—aye, smaller than the dust on a dead man’s tongue—what dance and jape with the sun’s own gold. When that great fiery doubloon climbs the mast o’ heaven, its light be a glitterin’ hoard o’ colors, but the blue-hearted sprites be the quickest cutlasses o’ the lot; they scatter the blue to every compass point, whilst the reds and yellows be left swabbin’ the deck. So the eye o’ any honest seadog—or e’en a landlubber such as yerself—drinks deep o’ that stolen blue, and the wide sky shows its true colors: a vast, brazen Caribbean o’ light, flyin’ high above our black-flagged world.
